cases.name: student_t_default_mu0_sigma1_nu10_moments
cases.note: Default constructor: mu=0, sigma=1, nu=10; Test_Construction, Test_Mean, Test_Median, Test_Mode, Test_StandardDeviation, Test_Skewness, Test_Kurtosis

cases.name: student_t_standard_nu4_pdf_cdf
cases.note: Test_StudentT_PDF, Test_StudentT_CDF: StudentT(nu=4) at x=1.4; C# uses mu=0,sigma=1,nu=4

cases.name: student_t_shifted_mu2p5_sigma0p5_nu4
cases.note: Test_StudentT_PDF, Test_StudentT_CDF: StudentT(2.5, 0.5, 4) at x=1.4. PDF re-pinned at v2.1.4/2a0357a (audited 1/sigma Jacobian fix): sigma=0.5 so the corrected density is exactly 2x the pre-fix 0.0516476521260042 (see docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md's StudentT PDF Jacobian entry, now resolved upstream).

cases.name: student_t_pdf_location_scale_identity
cases.note: Test_StudentT_PDF_LocationScaleIdentityAndNormalization: standard.PDF(z) / scaled.Sigma == scaled.PDF(x), where standard=StudentT(0,1,4), scaled=StudentT(2.5,0.5,4), x=1.4, z=(x-2.5)/0.5=-2.2. standard.PDF(-2.2) reproduces the OLD (pre-Jacobian-fix) literal 0.0516476521260042 exactly, since sigma=1 here is unaffected by the fix; pairing with student_t_shifted_mu2p5_sigma0p5_nu4's re-pinned 0.1032953042520084 = 0.0516476521260042 / 0.5 demonstrates the identity by inspection across the two cases. The C# test's own trapezoidal normalization check (100000-interval integral of scaled.PDF over +-100 sigma ~= 1) is not independently fixture-representable (no numeric-integration assertion in this schema); it exercises the same corrected pdf() this case and the shifted case already pin pointwise, so no separate oracle is added for it.

cases.name: student_t_nu1_undefined_moments
cases.note: Test_Mean, Test_Median, Test_Mode, Test_StandardDeviation, Test_Skewness: nu=1 edge cases

cases.name: student_t_nu2_infinite_sd
cases.note: Test_StandardDeviation: nu=2 -> sd=+Inf; Test_Kurtosis: nu<=2 -> kurtosis=NaN

cases.name: student_t_symmetry_tail_quantiles
cases.note: Symmetry: InverseCDF(1-p) = -InverseCDF(p) + 2*mu; tail quantiles exercise incomplete_inverse

cases.name: student_t_inverse_cdf_extreme_tail_retains_location_scale
cases.note: Test_StudentT_InverseCDF_ExtremeTailRetainsLocationAndScale: StudentT(mu=2, sigma=3, nu=1) at probability=1e-155 (nu=1 = Cauchy). v2.1.4/2a0357a removed the old `double.MaxValue * z < nu` overflow guard, which used to saturate to a bare +-double.MaxValue at this probability (ignoring mu/sigma entirely -- double.MaxValue*z with z~1e-310 subnormal is ~0.18 < nu=1, so the OLD guard fires here). The new t=sqrt(nu)*sqrt(1-z)/sqrt(z) form instead returns a finite value retaining mu+sigma*t, matching the closed-form Cauchy tail approximation mu - sigma/(pi*p) (exact to ~1e-16 relative at this probability, since the next correction term is O(p^2) -- astronomically below double precision). A second, less extreme probability (1e-150) confirms InverseCDF(1e-150) > InverseCDF(1e-155) (deeper tail probability -> more negative quantile), i.e. monotonicity is preserved through the refactor.

cases.name: student_t_large_df_normal_approx
cases.note: nu=1e8 >= 1e8 threshold: PDF/CDF degrade to standard normal approximation

cases.name: student_t_large_df_sigma2_normal_approx
cases.note: nu=1e8 large-df branch with sigma=2 (sigma!=1): PDF mirrors C# Normal.StandardPDF(Z) * (1/sigma) since v2.1.4/2a0357a's audited Jacobian fix. Re-pinned: both PDF values are exactly half the pre-fix literals (0.3989422804014327, 0.3520653267642995), an exact bit-halving since 1/sigma=0.5.

cases.name: student_t_invalid_params
cases.note: sigma<=0 or nu<1 -> parameters_valid = false; Test_InvalidParameters

cases.name: student_t_invalid_nu_nan
cases.note: v2.1.4 real fix (Harden distribution parameter validation): the old `v < 1.0d` check is false for NaN, so a NaN degrees-of-freedom silently read as valid before ValidateParameters gained an explicit isnan/isinf check -- see student_t.hpp's validate() note.

cases.name: student_t_invalid_nu_inf
cases.note: v2.1.4 real fix: `Infinity < 1.0d` is also false, so an infinite degrees-of-freedom silently read as valid before this same isnan/isinf check was added.
